title: pyrus keszthelyensis
intro: Pyrus keszthelyensis, also known as the Hungarian wild pear, is a species of wild pear tree native to the Pannonian region of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, derived from its fruit, leaves, and bark. The health benefits of Pyrus keszthelyensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. The plant is often prepared as herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
